_README: Question text for Q001-Q060. Text only - scoring lives in src/data/questions-scoring.json and is joined by id. Switching locale never changes scoring results.

questions.Q001: When a new intimate experience comes to mind, what tends to catch your attention first?
questions.Q002: How much does trust matter to you before exploring an intimate curiosity?
questions.Q003: When something sparks your curiosity, do you like to talk it through first or discover it little by little?
questions.Q004: How comfortable do you feel saying "no" or "not yet" in an intimate conversation?
questions.Q005: How appealing is it to set a special mood before an intimate experience?
questions.Q006: How strongly do you respond to visual details like style, clothing, presence, or attitude?
questions.Q007: How important are gentle touch, texture, or physical closeness to your intimate curiosity?
questions.Q008: Are you drawn more to a spontaneous experience or one planned with intention?
questions.Q009: How much does feeling desired increase your curiosity?
questions.Q010: How comfortable are you talking about a fantasy without feeling you have to act on it?
questions.Q011: Are you drawn to fantasies that play out like a story, a scene, or an imagined game?
questions.Q012: How appealing is it to bring out a more playful side of yourself in intimacy?
questions.Q013: How much does tenderness and care afterward matter to you in an intimate experience?
questions.Q014: How curious are you about dynamics where one person leads and the other follows, always with clear agreement?
questions.Q015: How important is having a word, signal, or agreement to pause an experience?
questions.Q016: How private are you with your intimate curiosities?
questions.Q017: Would you prefer your results stay just for you, even when using couple mode?
questions.Q018: How comfortable are you sharing curiosities if you can choose exactly what gets shared?
questions.Q019: Would you like the app to suggest conversation topics without revealing private details?
questions.Q020: How useful would it be to see where your curiosity and your partner's overlap?
questions.Q021: How comfortable are you if your partner has a curiosity you don't share?
questions.Q022: When you and your partner don't match on a curiosity, do you prefer to close the topic or understand it calmly?
questions.Q023: How important is it that a shared curiosity starts off gently and gradually?
questions.Q024: Would you like the couple result to focus on common ground before differences?
questions.Q025: How important is it that the app marks firm boundaries as non-negotiable zones?
questions.Q026: Have you ever felt curious about something only in your imagination, without wanting to act on it?
questions.Q027: Would you like a private space to sort out your curiosities before talking them over with someone?
questions.Q028: How clear are you on the difference between "I'm curious" and "I want to try it"?
questions.Q029: How comfortable are you exploring intimate topics in writing before talking about them out loud?
questions.Q030: How important is it that the other person validates your desire without mocking or judging?
questions.Q031: Are you drawn more to feeling admired, cared for, challenged, or surprised?
questions.Q032: How present is aesthetics in your desire, like lighting, clothing, music, or atmosphere?
questions.Q033: How important are sound, voice, or way of speaking in your intimate curiosity?
questions.Q034: Are you interested in more direct conversations about what you like and don't like?
questions.Q035: How easy is it for you to ask for what you want without feeling embarrassed?
questions.Q036: How appealing is it for an experience to hold surprise, without losing clear agreements?
questions.Q037: How comfortable are you trying something new if you know you can stop it at any moment?
questions.Q038: How important is talking afterward about how an intimate experience felt?
questions.Q039: Are you interested in exploring desires that don't change who you are or how you label yourself?
questions.Q040: How fluid or changing does your curiosity feel over time?
questions.Q041: When you think about attraction, do you distinguish between physical, emotional, romantic, and fantasy attraction?
questions.Q042: Is it easier for you to talk about curiosity in terms of energy, connection, or identity?
questions.Q043: How comfortable are you with a fantasy not having to define your orientation?
questions.Q044: How important is it for you to keep some fantasies as part of your private world?
questions.Q045: How comfortable are you receiving intimate questions if the language is respectful and not graphic?
questions.Q046: Would you prefer a short, gentle quiz or a deeper, more detailed one?
questions.Q047: How important is it that you can skip questions without negatively affecting your result?
questions.Q048: Are you more interested in conversation ideas than action suggestions?
questions.Q049: How open are you to learning new vocabulary to describe your desires?
questions.Q050: Would you like the result to identify "zones of curiosity" without calling them fetishes?
questions.Q051: How appealing is it to feel you have permission to explore at your own pace?
questions.Q052: How important is it that a partner doesn't use your answers to pressure you afterward?
questions.Q053: Would you like the couple report to include phrases for starting difficult conversations?
questions.Q054: How comfortable are you if the app only shows overlaps and hides private differences?
questions.Q055: How important is it that you both answer separately before seeing shared results?
questions.Q056: Are you interested in finding areas where you both feel curiosity, even if you don't want to explore anything yet?
questions.Q057: How comfortable are you talking about boundaries before talking about fantasies?
questions.Q058: How important is it that curiosity gets explored with humor, lightness, and zero judgment?
questions.Q059: Would you like the app to help you tell apart desire, pressure, fear, and genuine curiosity?
questions.Q060: After seeing your result, would you prefer a brief summary, a deep report, or a conversation guide?
